fr:title.text: Modes of metavariables in type judgements

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Modes of metavariables in type judgements

fr:mainmatter.fr:p:
Automated deduction and logic programming can be a useful lens through which to view type systems. Given the typing judgement \Gamma \vdash e : A with metavariables \Gamma , e and A, we can treat each of these metavariables as either inputs or outputs:
If all three are inputs, then we are speaking of type checking.
If the type A is an output, then we are speaking of type synthesis.
If the term e is an output, then we are speaking of program synthesis.
The "input" or "output" status of each metavariables is its mode.
